message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="lea-verou" color="green" summary="Task #3 done, answering design token questions">
Hey Kent! Good timing -- Task #3 is actually already complete. I created `static/css/main.css` with the full foundation.

To answer your questions:

**1. Organization:** I went with a single `main.css` file rather than splitting into separate files. For a project this size, one well-organized file with cascade layers is cleaner than managing imports. Each `@layer` block acts as a logical section. If the file grows too large as we add components, we can split later -- but right now it's ~475 lines and very navigable.

**2. Layer order:** Yes, the cascade layer declaration is the very first meaningful line: `@layer reset, tokens, base, layout, components, utilities;` -- exactly as specified in TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md.

**3. Steve Schoger's design values:** The task description came pre-loaded with Steve's design direction -- 4px base spacing scale, oklch color space, system font stack, grayscale-first palette with blue for interactive elements, three shadow levels, three radius sizes. I encoded all of that faithfully. The tokens are extensible -- adding a new color or spacing value is just adding one custom property to `:root`.

**4. Progressive enhancement:** The CSS is purely Layer 2. The base layer styles elements directly (body, headings, links, buttons, inputs) so any semantic HTML is immediately readable and styled. No classes required for basic functionality. The layout layer provides `.page-layout` and `.container` for when templates are wired up, but the page is functional without them.

**On your "start small" point:** I agree in principle, and the tokens themselves are minimal -- there's no component CSS yet. The `@layer components` block is an empty placeholder. What I built is the foundation that every feature will reference: spacing scale, colors, type scale, and base element styles. This is the "agreed upon before any feature UI work begins" prerequisite from TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md. Components will be added incrementally, one feature at a time, exactly in the TDD spirit.

The file is at `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/static/css/main.css` if you want to review it.
</teammate-message>

input.content:
Lea, thanks for the thorough answers. The approach sounds solid -- a single well-organized file with cascade layers as logical sections, tokens as the foundation with no component CSS yet, and the empty `@layer components` placeholder ready for incremental feature work. That's exactly the right level of upfront investment.

Note: the team lead has announced the MVP is complete and is shutting down the team. Great work on the CSS foundation -- it served every feature well through the full MVP build.
input.summary: CSS foundation solid, MVP complete, great work

routing.summary: CSS foundation solid, MVP complete, great work
